verses.text: 'Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory,' for His mercy and truth's sake, while the heathen ask, 'Where is now their God?' He is in the heavens, doing as He pleases. Glory belongs to God alone. The believer seeks not his own honour but his Maker's.

'Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory,' for His mercy and truth's sake, while the heathen ask, 'Where is now their God?' He is in the heavens, doing as He pleases. Glory belongs to God alone. The believer seeks not his own honour but his Maker's.

'Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory,' for His mercy and truth's sake, while the heathen ask, 'Where is now their God?' He is in the heavens, doing as He pleases. Glory belongs to God alone. The believer seeks not his own honour but his Maker's.

The nations' idols are silver and gold, with mouths, eyes, and ears that cannot work; 'they that make them are like unto them.' Dead gods make dead worshippers. We grow like what we worship, whether lifeless idols or the living God.

The nations' idols are silver and gold, with mouths, eyes, and ears that cannot work; 'they that make them are like unto them.' Dead gods make dead worshippers. We grow like what we worship, whether lifeless idols or the living God.

Israel, the priests, and all who fear the LORD are urged to trust Him, 'their help and their shield.' Trust is the answer to idols. Against worthless images stands the living God who helps and defends.

The LORD has been mindful of His people and will bless them, both small and great, who fear Him. None is too lowly for His care. God remembers and blesses every one of His own.
The LORD has been mindful of His people and will bless them, both small and great, who fear Him. None is too lowly for His care. God remembers and blesses every one of His own.
He will increase His people more and more, who are 'blessed of the LORD which made heaven and earth.' Blessing flows from the Creator. The God of all power delights to multiply good to His people.
He will increase His people more and more, who are 'blessed of the LORD which made heaven and earth.' Blessing flows from the Creator. The God of all power delights to multiply good to His people.

The heavens are the LORD's, but the earth He has given to men; the dead are silent, 'but we will bless the LORD' for evermore. The living must praise now. While we have breath, let us bless the God who gave it.
